red.name: Ile de France
red.care: Full sun, well-drained soil, water weekly
red.fact: One of the most planted tulips at the Ottawa festival. Red tulips symbolize true love — during Tulip Mania in 1637 Holland, a single rare bulb sold for the price of a house.

yellow.name: Strong Gold
yellow.care: Full sun, sandy soil, drought tolerant
yellow.fact: Yellow tulips represent cheerfulness and sunshine. They were first cultivated in the Ottoman Empire before Dutch traders brought them to Europe in the 1500s.

purple.name: Negrita
purple.care: Partial shade tolerant, rich moist soil
purple.fact: Negrita is one of the most award-winning purple tulips in the world. Purple tulips symbolize royalty — they were historically reserved for nobility.

white.name: Pays Bas
white.fact: White tulips hold special meaning — the Netherlands gifted 100,000 bulbs to Canada in 1945 to thank us for sheltering the Dutch royal family during WW2.

pink.name: Prominence
pink.fact: Double Early tulips are called Peony tulips because their full layered blooms resemble peonies. Pink tulips mean affection and are the most photographed at the festival.

orange.name: Orange Emperor
orange.care: Full sun, dry to medium soil
orange.fact: Orange Emperor is one of the earliest tulips to bloom each spring. Orange tulips represent energy and enthusiasm — they are a favourite of the Dutch royal family.
